trip.name: Hong Kong Food and Culture Experience

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Culinary delights and city exploration

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong Airport
timeline.detail: Clearing customs and heading to the city. Follow the signs to the Airport Express for easy transport to Central Station.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to The Salisbury – YMCA of Hong Kong
timeline.detail: Settle in at this waterfront hotel located near Tsim Sha Tsui and close to the MTR station. Stunning views of Victoria Harbour await.
timeline.mapQuery: The Salisbury – YMCA of Hong Kong, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Stroll along the promenade and enjoy breathtaking views of the skyline across Victoria Harbour. Great for photography and relaxation.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic Cantonese wonton noodles at this famed eatery. Find out why locals argue it's some of the best in town.
timeline.mapQuery: Mak's Noodle, G/F, 77 Wellington St, Central, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch Hong Kong's iconic light and sound show featuring a stunning display across the skyline. Arrive early for a good spot.

timeline.title: Nightcap at Ozone Bar
timeline.detail: End your day with drinks at one of the highest bars in the world, located on the 118th floor of the Ritz-Carlton. Amazing views guaranteed!

localTips: Purchase an Octopus Card at the airport for easy public transport access.
Try to catch the Symphony of Lights from the Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront for the best views.
Street food is a must—don't miss out on the egg waffles and mango pudding!
Book your dining spots in advance at popular restaurants.
Consider visiting the Sky100 observation deck for even more impressive sights.

days.title: Cultural Exploration in Kowloon
days.subtitle: Markets, museums, and temples

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australian Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a classic Hong Kong breakfast—scrambled eggs, toast, and milk tea at this beloved diner.
timeline.mapQuery: Australian Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore this famous temple known for its striking architecture and vibrant atmosphere. A great place to learn about local religious customs.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Wong Tai Sin, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Indulge in dim sum at the Michelin-starred dim sum restaurant. Be sure to try the baked buns and shrimp dumplings!
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Sham Shui Po, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Sham Shui Po Markets
timeline.detail: Wander through this bustling area known for fabric markets, electronics, and street food stalls. Ideal for local shopping experiences.

timeline.title: Visit the Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Learn about the rich history of Hong Kong from its early days to the present through engaging exhibits and artifacts.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Yung Kee Restaurant
timeline.detail: Savor some of Hong Kong's famous roast goose at this iconic restaurant. Be prepared for a bustling yet rewarding dining experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Yung Kee Restaurant, Central, Hong Kong

localTips: Use MTR to travel between locations; it's efficient and easy.
Try to visit Wong Tai Sin Temple early to avoid crowds.
Don’t forget to bargain at Sham Shui Po markets!
Paid tours are available for a deeper insight into the history museums.
Avoid peak dining hours in popular restaurants to minimize wait times.

days.title: Lantau Island Adventure
days.subtitle: Nature and tranquility

timeline.title: Breakfast at hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a continental breakfast at the hotel to fuel up for the day ahead. A perfect start before heading out.
timeline.mapQuery: The Salisbury – YMCA of Hong Kong, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car
timeline.detail: Enjoy stunning views of Lantau Island as you ascend in the Ngong Ping 360 cable car. A great way to start your adventure.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping 360, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Tian Tan Buddha
timeline.detail: Explore the remarkable Tian Tan Buddha statue and the Po Lin Monastery. The atmosphere is peaceful, and the views are splendid.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery
timeline.detail: Savor a vegetarian meal at the Po Lin Monastery dining hall, known for its healthy and delicious offerings.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Lantau Island Beaches
timeline.detail: Relax at one of Lantau Island's beautiful beaches—repulse bay or Cheung Sha beach. Unwind and enjoy the scenery.
timeline.mapQuery: Cheung Sha Beach, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hong Kong city
timeline.detail: Take the MTR back from Lantau Island, enjoy the scenic views once more before heading back to the bustling city.

timeline.title: Farewell Dinner at Jumbo Kingdom
timeline.detail: Savor seafood dishes at the iconic floating restaurant, Jumbo Kingdom. A unique dining experience that should not be missed.

localTips: Check the weather before visiting Lantau Island; it can be quite different from the city.
Find out the cable car schedule to avoid long wait times.
Bring swimwear if you plan to relax on the beach.
Make a reservation at Jumbo Kingdom to ensure you have a spot.
Consider carrying cash for smaller vendors and restaurants.

hotels.name: The Salisbury – YMCA of Hong Kong
hotels.note: Located in Tsim Sha Tsui with beautiful harbour views.
hotels.address: 41 Salisbury Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip fare for the cable car

urgent.label: Book Ngong Ping 360 ticket in advance
urgent.note: Tickets can sell out quickly; buy early.

urgent.label: Reserve table at Jumbo Kingdom
urgent.note: Book ahead for dinner at floating restaurant.
